title: Run Support Ops
subtitle: A small starting shape for support work where request flow and operator tooling matter first.
scope: One support or operations domain where requests enter through a mediated path and operators need a clear tool layer.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder request paths, workflow references, and tooling paths with real support sources.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when support work is still simple and you mainly need to show how requests flow and which tools operators depend on.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Start with the request path and operator tool layer first; only add richer operational surfaces when the support system stops being legible in that minimal form.
keepDistinct: later operational surfaces that may be added as complexity grows
notFor: full support-program inventories on day one

diagram.mermaid:
flowchart LR status_snapshot["status-snapshot
Summarize support readiness without carrying request-flow details."] operation["operation
Describe how requests enter and move through the support domain."] tooling_surface["tooling-surface
Expose trusted tools used by operators to run or inspect the request flow."] status_snapshot -- "summarizes" --> operation tooling_surface -- "supports" --> operation

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Seed an operations doc with request flow and the operator tooling that supports it.
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A small starting shape for support work where request flow and operator tooling matter first.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: One support or operations domain where requests enter through a mediated path and operators need a clear tool layer.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Make the request path and operator tool layer legible enough that someone can quickly understand how the support domain currently runs.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: A reader can see how a request enters the system, who or what fulfills it, and which tools the operators trust.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.role: Summarize support readiness without carrying request-flow details.
sections.rationale: This section keeps operational status visible while request flow and tool support remain inspectable.

sections.title: Mediated Operation
sections.role: Describe how requests enter and move through the support domain.
sections.rationale: The mediated operation is the core of the support domain because it names how work enters, is fulfilled, and moves next.

sections.title: Tooling Surface
sections.role: Expose trusted tools used by operators to run or inspect the request flow.
sections.rationale: Operators need visible tooling so the support flow can be run without rediscovering scripts or workflows.

relationships.rationale: Support status should summarize the actual mediated operation.
A request flow is operational only when operators can see the trusted tools that support it.

stageSignals.question: What first request path or mediated operation makes this support domain tangible?

stageSignals.when: operation exists but tooling-surface has no trusted tool card
stageSignals.question: Which tool does the operator trust to run, inspect, or update this support flow?
Can a reader understand how requests enter and which tools operators trust?

validOperations.label: Add operation card
validOperations.description: Add a request-flow card naming the intake path, current support, and next step.

validOperations.label: Add operator tooling
validOperations.description: Add the trusted script, workflow, or tool used by operators.

objective: Make the request path and operator tool layer legible enough that someone can quickly understand how the support domain currently runs.
successCondition: A reader can see how a request enters the system, who or what fulfills it, and which tools the operators trust.

callouts.title: How to use this starter
items: Focus first on the main request flow rather than trying to catalogue every support lane.
Add operating surfaces and enablers once the support model starts branching.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Starter intent
items: Keep the top of the document about operational reality, not background context.
The detailed request flow belongs in the operation section below.

sections.rationale: This section keeps operational status visible while request flow and tool support remain inspectable.

sections.title: Mediated Operation

data.name: Incoming request intake
data.currentSupport: Operators can accept structured requests and route them into a known fulfillment path.
data.nextStep: Add stronger validation for underspecified requests before scaling the flow further.
notes: Write the main request path first. You can branch into more surfaces later.

sections.rationale: The mediated operation is the core of the support domain because it names how work enters, is fulfilled, and moves next.

sections.title: Tooling Surface

data.name: Support router
data.purpose: Normalize incoming support requests and route them into the right operator lane.
data.whenToUse: Use when manual triage starts becoming the main source of delay.
caveats: Prototype routing logic should not be treated as authoritative until its failure modes are understood.

sections.rationale: Operators need visible tooling so the support flow can be run without rediscovering scripts or workflows.
